Class.Documentation:
Restricts text values to a predefined set of allowed values.

Example: color can be one of {red, blue, green}

Persistence config example: {"allowedValues": ["red", "blue", "green"]}
Class.Name: AllowedValuesConstraint

Class.Documentation:
Context for evaluating applicability constraints.

Generic key-value map representing the situation in which we check applicability.
Class.Documentation_plain: Context for evaluating applicability constraints. Generic key-value map representing the situation in which we check applicability.
Class.Name: ApplicabilityContext

Class.Documentation:
Batch describes a set of ProductInstances of a specific ProductType

that are tracked together, usually for quality control purposes.

Used when:

- Individual instance identity is unimportant, but batch origin matters

- Need to track manufacturing/quality control information

- Common with foodstuffs, chemicals, and manufactured goods

Examples: food batches with expiry dates, manufactured parts from same production run
Class.Documentation_plain:
Batch describes a set of ProductInstances of a specific ProductType that are tracked together, usually for quality control purposes.
Used when: - Individual instance identity is unimportant, but batch origin matters - Need to track manufacturing/quality control information - Common with foodstuffs, chemicals, and manufactured goods
Examples: food batches with expiry dates, manufactured parts from same production run
Class.Name: Batch

Class.Documentation:
CatalogEntry - commercial offering position.

Represents what the organization currently offers to customers.

Product (ProductType or PackageType) says what something IS (business/operational definition).

CatalogEntry says that something is FOR SALE (commercial availability).

Key differences from Product:

- displayName: marketing name (vs technical name)

- description: sales copy (vs technical description)

- categories: for navigation/search

- validity: when it's available for purchase

- metadata: flexible attributes (featured, badges, promotions, etc.)
Class.Documentation_plain:
CatalogEntry - commercial offering position. Represents what the organization currently offers to customers.
Product (ProductType or PackageType) says what something IS (business/operational definition). CatalogEntry says that something is FOR SALE (commercial availability).
Key differences from Product: - displayName: marketing name (vs technical name) - description: sales copy (vs technical description) - categories: for navigation/search - validity: when it's available for purchase - metadata: flexible attributes (featured, badges, promotions, etc.)
Class.Name: CatalogEntry

Operation.Documentation: Checks if entry is available for purchase at given date.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if entry is available for purchase at given date.
Operation.Name: isAvailableAt

Operation.Documentation: Checks if entry belongs to given category.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if entry belongs to given category.
Operation.Name: isInCategory

Operation.Documentation: Returns metadata value for given key.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns metadata value for given key.
Operation.Name: getMetadata

Operation.Documentation: Returns metadata value or default if not present.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns metadata value or default if not present.
Operation.Name: getMetadataOrDefault

Operation.Documentation: Checks if metadata key exists.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if metadata key exists.
Operation.Name: hasMetadata

Operation.Documentation: Creates a copy with updated validity (for discontinuation).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a copy with updated validity (for discontinuation).
Operation.Name: withValidity

Operation.Documentation: Creates a copy with updated metadata.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a copy with updated metadata.
Operation.Name: withMetadata

Class.Documentation:
Unique identifier for a CatalogEntry (may differ from ProductIdentifier).

The same ProductType can appear in multiple catalog entries:

- Different marketing campaigns

- Different markets/regions

- Different time periods
Class.Documentation_plain:
Unique identifier for a CatalogEntry (may differ from ProductIdentifier).
The same ProductType can appear in multiple catalog entries: - Different marketing campaigns - Different markets/regions - Different time periods
Class.Name: CatalogEntryId

Class.Documentation: Repository for CatalogEntry persistence.
Class.Documentation_plain: Repository for CatalogEntry persistence.
Class.Name: CatalogEntryRepository

Class.Documentation: In-memory implementation of CatalogEntryRepository.
Class.Documentation_plain: In-memory implementation of CatalogEntryRepository.
Class.Name: InMemoryCatalogEntryRepository

Class.Documentation:
Restricts date values to a date range [from, to].

Example: expiry date between 2024-01-01 and 2024-12-31

Persistence config example: {"from": "2024-01-01", "to": "2024-12-31"}
Class.Name: DateRangeConstraint

Class.Documentation:
Restricts decimal values to a numeric range [min, max].

Example: weight between 0.5 and 100.0 kg

Persistence config example: {"min": "0.5", "max": "100.0"}
Class.Name: DecimalRangeConstraint

Class.Documentation:
Defines constraints on product feature values, including type validation and value validation.

Each constraint:

- Specifies the expected value type (TEXT, INTEGER, DECIMAL, DATE, BOOLEAN)

- Has a string identifier for persistence/deserialization

- Validates whether a given value satisfies the constraint

- Provides conversion between objects and String representation for persistence

- Describes the constraint in human-readable form

Implementations include:

- AllowedValuesConstraint: restricts to a set of allowed values

- NumericRangeConstraint: restricts integers to a range

- DecimalRangeConstraint: restricts decimals to a range

- RegexConstraint: validates text against a regex pattern

- DateRangeConstraint: restricts dates to a range

- UnconstrainedConstraint: accepts any value of the specified type
Class.Documentation_plain:
Defines constraints on product feature values, including type validation and value validation.
Each constraint: - Specifies the expected value type (TEXT, INTEGER, DECIMAL, DATE, BOOLEAN) - Has a string identifier for persistence/deserialization - Validates whether a given value satisfies the constraint - Provides conversion between objects and String representation for persistence - Describes the constraint in human-readable form
Implementations include: - AllowedValuesConstraint: restricts to a set of allowed values - NumericRangeConstraint: restricts integers to a range - DecimalRangeConstraint: restricts decimals to a range - RegexConstraint: validates text against a regex pattern - DateRangeConstraint: restricts dates to a range - UnconstrainedConstraint: accepts any value of the specified type
Class.Name: FeatureValueConstraint

Operation.Documentation: Returns the type of values this constraint applies to.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the type of values this constraint applies to.
Operation.Name: valueType

Operation.Documentation:
Returns the constraint type identifier for persistence/deserialization.

Examples: "ALLOWED_VALUES", "NUMERIC_RANGE", "REGEX", "DATE_RANGE", "UNCONSTRAINED"
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the constraint type identifier for persistence/deserialization. Examples: "ALLOWED_VALUES", "NUMERIC_RANGE", "REGEX", "DATE_RANGE", "UNCONSTRAINED"
Operation.Name: type

Operation.Documentation:
Validates whether the given value satisfies this constraint.

The value must be of the correct type (checked via valueType()) and meet

the constraint's specific requirements.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Validates whether the given value satisfies this constraint. The value must be of the correct type (checked via valueType()) and meet the constraint's specific requirements.
Operation.Name: isValid
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: true if the value is valid, false otherwise
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: true if the value is valid, false otherwise

Operation.Documentation:
Returns a human-readable description of this constraint.

Example: "one of: {red, blue, green}" or "integer between 1 and 100"
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns a human-readable description of this constraint. Example: "one of: {red, blue, green}" or "integer between 1 and 100"
Operation.Name: desc

Operation.Documentation:
Converts the object to its String representation for persistence.

Uses the valueType's conversion logic.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Converts the object to its String representation for persistence. Uses the valueType's conversion logic.
Operation.Name: toString

Parameter.Documentation: the value to convert (must be valid)
Parameter.Documentation_plain: the value to convert (must be valid)
Parameter.Name: value

Operation.Documentation:
Converts a String representation to the object, applying validation.

Uses the valueType's conversion logic and then validates the result.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Converts a String representation to the object, applying validation. Uses the valueType's conversion logic and then validates the result.
Operation.Name: fromString
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation:
the converted and validated object

@throws IllegalArgumentException if the value cannot be parsed or is invalid
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: the converted and validated object @throws IllegalArgumentException if the value cannot be parsed or is invalid

Class.Documentation:
Defines the safe set of data types that can be used for product feature values.

Each type knows how to convert between its runtime representation and String (for persistence).

This enum restricts feature values to a well-defined set of types, preventing

arbitrary classes from being used as feature values.
Class.Documentation_plain:
Defines the safe set of data types that can be used for product feature values. Each type knows how to convert between its runtime representation and String (for persistence).
This enum restricts feature values to a well-defined set of types, preventing arbitrary classes from being used as feature values.
Class.Name: FeatureValueType

Operation.Documentation: Converts a String representation to the runtime type.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Converts a String representation to the runtime type.
Operation.Name: castFrom
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws IllegalArgumentException if the value cannot be parsed
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws IllegalArgumentException if the value cannot be parsed

Operation.Documentation: Converts the runtime type to its String representation (for persistence).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Converts the runtime type to its String representation (for persistence).
Operation.Name: castTo

Operation.Documentation: Returns the class that represents this value type.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the class that represents this value type.
Operation.Name: type

Operation.Documentation: Checks if the given value is an instance of this type.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if the given value is an instance of this type.
Operation.Name: isInstance

Class.Documentation:
Instance represents a specific instance/exemplar of a Product (either ProductType or PackageType).

While Product defines WHAT can be sold, Instance represents WHAT WAS actually sold/created.

<p>

Common interface for:

- ProductInstance: specific instance of a ProductType (e.g., iPhone with serial ABC123)

- PackageInstance: specific instance of a PackageType (e.g., laptop bundle with customer's choices)

<p>

Every instance must be tracked by at least one of:

- SerialNumber (individual tracking)

- Batch (group tracking for quality control)

The tracking requirements are defined by the Product's tracking strategy.
Class.Documentation_plain:
Instance represents a specific instance/exemplar of a Product (either ProductType or PackageType). While Product defines WHAT can be sold, Instance represents WHAT WAS actually sold/created.

Common interface for: - ProductInstance: specific instance of a ProductType (e.g., iPhone with serial ABC123) - PackageInstance: specific instance of a PackageType (e.g., laptop bundle with customer's choices)

Every instance must be tracked by at least one of: - SerialNumber (individual tracking) - Batch (group tracking for quality control) The tracking requirements are defined by the Product's tracking strategy.
Class.Name: Instance

Class.Documentation:
Builder for creating both ProductInstance and PackageInstance with fluent API.

<p>

Common attributes (id, serial, batch) are set in the main builder.

Type-specific attributes are set in specialized inner builders returned by asProductInstance() or asPackageInstance().

<p>

Usage:

<pre>

ProductInstance instance = new InstanceBuilder(InstanceId.newOne())

.withSerial(SerialNumber.of("ABC123"))

.asProductInstance(laptopType)

.withQuantity(Quantity.of(1, Unit.pieces()))

.withFeature(colorFeature, "silver")

.build();

PackageInstance packageInstance = new InstanceBuilder(InstanceId.newOne())

.withBatch(BatchId.of("BATCH-001"))

.asPackageInstance(bundleType)

.withSelection(List.of(

new SelectedInstance(ram16GBInstance, 1),

new SelectedInstance(ssd512GBInstance, 1)

))

.build();

</pre>
Class.Documentation_plain:
Builder for creating both ProductInstance and PackageInstance with fluent API.

Common attributes (id, serial, batch) are set in the main builder. Type-specific attributes are set in specialized inner builders returned by asProductInstance() or asPackageInstance().

Usage: 

ProductInstance instance = new InstanceBuilder(InstanceId.newOne())
.withSerial(SerialNumber.of("ABC123"))
.asProductInstance(laptopType)
.withQuantity(Quantity.of(1, Unit.pieces()))
.withFeature(colorFeature, "silver")
.build();

PackageInstance packageInstance = new InstanceBuilder(InstanceId.newOne())
.withBatch(BatchId.of("BATCH-001"))
.asPackageInstance(bundleType)
.withSelection(List.of(
new SelectedInstance(ram16GBInstance, 1),
new SelectedInstance(ssd512GBInstance, 1)
))
.build();
Class.Name: InstanceBuilder

Operation.Documentation: Sets serial number for the instance.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Sets serial number for the instance.
Operation.Name: withSerial

Operation.Documentation: Sets batch ID for the instance.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Sets batch ID for the instance.
Operation.Name: withBatch

Operation.Documentation:
Starts building a ProductInstance.

Returns specialized builder for ProductInstance-specific attributes.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Starts building a ProductInstance. Returns specialized builder for ProductInstance-specific attributes.
Operation.Name: asProductInstance

Operation.Documentation:
Starts building a PackageInstance.

Returns specialized builder for PackageInstance-specific attributes.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Starts building a PackageInstance. Returns specialized builder for PackageInstance-specific attributes.
Operation.Name: asPackageInstance

Class.Documentation:
Specialized builder for ProductInstance.

Has access to common fields from outer InstanceBuilder.
Class.Documentation_plain: Specialized builder for ProductInstance. Has access to common fields from outer InstanceBuilder.
Class.Name: ProductInstanceBuilder

Class.Documentation:
Specialized builder for PackageInstance.

Has access to common fields from outer InstanceBuilder.
Class.Documentation_plain: Specialized builder for PackageInstance. Has access to common fields from outer InstanceBuilder.
Class.Name: PackageInstanceBuilder

Class.Documentation:
Restricts integer values to a numeric range [min, max].

Example: year of production between 2020 and 2024

Persistence config example: {"min": 2020, "max": 2024}
Class.Name: NumericRangeConstraint

Class.Documentation:
PackageInstance represents a specific instance of a PackageType with concrete instances delivered.

Examples:

- PackageType: "Laptop Bundle" -> PackageInstance: delivered laptop SN-ABC, RAM batch-001, SSD SN-XYZ

- PackageType: "Banking Package" -> PackageInstance: delivered Gold card SN-123, insurance policy POL-456

PackageInstance tracks:

- Which PackageType was purchased

- Concrete instances delivered (ProductInstance or PackageInstance)

- Optional serial number or batch for the package itself

The delivered instances are validated against the PackageType's structure to ensure all rules are satisfied.
Class.Documentation_plain:
PackageInstance represents a specific instance of a PackageType with concrete instances delivered.
Examples: - PackageType: "Laptop Bundle" -> PackageInstance: delivered laptop SN-ABC, RAM batch-001, SSD SN-XYZ - PackageType: "Banking Package" -> PackageInstance: delivered Gold card SN-123, insurance policy POL-456
PackageInstance tracks: - Which PackageType was purchased - Concrete instances delivered (ProductInstance or PackageInstance) - Optional serial number or batch for the package itself
The delivered instances are validated against the PackageType's structure to ensure all rules are satisfied.
Class.Name: PackageInstance

Class.Documentation:
PackageStructure defines what products can be in a package and how they can be combined.

<p>

It consists of two parts:

1. ProductSets - collections of available products (the "raw material")

2. SelectionRules - rules defining valid selections (the "constraints")

<p>

Think of ProductSets as ingredients available in a kitchen, and SelectionRules

as the recipe rules: "use 1 meat", "add 2-3 vegetables", "if chicken then must add sauce".
Class.Documentation_plain:
PackageStructure defines what products can be in a package and how they can be combined.

It consists of two parts: 1. ProductSets - collections of available products (the "raw material") 2. SelectionRules - rules defining valid selections (the "constraints")

Think of ProductSets as ingredients available in a kitchen, and SelectionRules as the recipe rules: "use 1 meat", "add 2-3 vegetables", "if chicken then must add sauce".
Class.Name: PackageStructure

Operation.Documentation: Validates if customer's product selection satisfies all package rules.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Validates if customer's product selection satisfies all package rules.
Operation.Name: validate

Class.Documentation:
PackageType represents a product composed of other products.

<p>

Examples:

- Laptop Bundle (laptop + bag + mouse + insurance)

- Banking Package (account + card + insurance)

- Office Setup (hardware package + software package + support)

<p>

PackageType can contain other PackageTypes (nested packages), forming a composite structure.

This is the composite in the composite pattern.
Class.Documentation_plain:
PackageType represents a product composed of other products.

Examples: - Laptop Bundle (laptop + bag + mouse + insurance) - Banking Package (account + card + insurance) - Office Setup (hardware package + software package + support)

PackageType can contain other PackageTypes (nested packages), forming a composite structure. This is the composite in the composite pattern.
Class.Name: PackageType

Operation.Documentation: Creates a package with default settings (UNIQUE tracking, no applicability constraints).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a package with default settings (UNIQUE tracking, no applicability constraints).
Operation.Name: define

Operation.Documentation: Validates if selected products match package structure rules.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Validates if selected products match package structure rules.
Operation.Name: validateSelection

Class.Documentation:
Product - component in composite pattern.

Can be either ProductType (leaf - regular product) or PackageType (composite - package of products).
Class.Documentation_plain: Product - component in composite pattern. Can be either ProductType (leaf - regular product) or PackageType (composite - package of products).
Class.Name: Product

Class.Documentation:
Builder for creating both ProductType and PackageType with fluent API.

<p>

Common attributes (id, name, description, metadata, applicability) are set in the main builder.

Type-specific attributes are set in specialized inner builders returned by asProductType() or asPackage().

<p>

Usage:

<pre>

ProductType laptop = new ProductBuilder(id, name, description)

.withMetadata("category", "electronics")

.asProductType(Unit.pieces(), ProductTrackingStrategy.INDIVIDUALLY_TRACKED)

.withMandatoryFeature(colorFeature)

.build();

PackageType bundle = new ProductBuilder(id, name, description)

.withMetadata("promotion", "summer2025")

.asPackage()

.withSingleChoice("Memory", ram8GB.id(), ram16GB.id())

.withSingleChoice("Storage", ssd256GB.id(), ssd512GB.id())

.withOptionalChoice("Accessories", mouse.id(), bag.id())

.build();

</pre>
Class.Documentation_plain:
Builder for creating both ProductType and PackageType with fluent API.

Common attributes (id, name, description, metadata, applicability) are set in the main builder. Type-specific attributes are set in specialized inner builders returned by asProductType() or asPackage().

Usage: 

ProductType laptop = new ProductBuilder(id, name, description)
.withMetadata("category", "electronics")
.asProductType(Unit.pieces(), ProductTrackingStrategy.INDIVIDUALLY_TRACKED)
.withMandatoryFeature(colorFeature)
.build();

PackageType bundle = new ProductBuilder(id, name, description)
.withMetadata("promotion", "summer2025")
.asPackage()
.withSingleChoice("Memory", ram8GB.id(), ram16GB.id())
.withSingleChoice("Storage", ssd256GB.id(), ssd512GB.id())
.withOptionalChoice("Accessories", mouse.id(), bag.id())
.build();
Class.Name: ProductBuilder

Operation.Documentation: Sets metadata for the product/package.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Sets metadata for the product/package.
Operation.Name: withMetadata

Operation.Documentation: Adds a single metadata entry.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Adds a single metadata entry.
Operation.Name: withMetadata

Operation.Documentation: Sets applicability constraint for the product/package.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Sets applicability constraint for the product/package.
Operation.Name: withApplicabilityConstraint

Operation.Documentation:
Starts building a ProductType (regular product).

Returns specialized builder for ProductType-specific attributes.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Starts building a ProductType (regular product). Returns specialized builder for ProductType-specific attributes.
Operation.Name: asProductType

Operation.Documentation:
Starts building a PackageType (package of products).

Returns specialized builder with fluent API for defining package structure.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Starts building a PackageType (package of products). Returns specialized builder with fluent API for defining package structure.
Operation.Name: asPackageType

Class.Documentation:
Specialized builder for ProductType.

Has access to common fields from outer ProductBuilder.
Class.Documentation_plain: Specialized builder for ProductType. Has access to common fields from outer ProductBuilder.
Class.Name: ProductTypeBuilder

Class.Documentation:
Specialized builder for PackageType with fluent API for defining package structure.

Has access to common fields from outer ProductBuilder.
Class.Documentation_plain: Specialized builder for PackageType with fluent API for defining package structure. Has access to common fields from outer ProductBuilder.
Class.Name: PackageTypeBuilder

Operation.Documentation:
Adds a product set with "select exactly one" rule.

Example: customer must choose exactly 1 memory option from the set.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Adds a product set with "select exactly one" rule. Example: customer must choose exactly 1 memory option from the set.
Operation.Name: withSingleChoice

Operation.Documentation:
Adds a product set with "select zero or one" rule.

Example: customer may optionally choose 1 accessory from the set.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Adds a product set with "select zero or one" rule. Example: customer may optionally choose 1 accessory from the set.
Operation.Name: withOptionalChoice

Operation.Documentation:
Adds a product set with "select at least one" rule.

Example: customer must choose at least 1 item from the set (can choose more).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Adds a product set with "select at least one" rule. Example: customer must choose at least 1 item from the set (can choose more).
Operation.Name: withRequiredChoice

Operation.Documentation:
Adds a product set with custom min/max selection rule.

Example: customer must choose 2-4 items from the set.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Adds a product set with custom min/max selection rule. Example: customer must choose 2-4 items from the set.
Operation.Name: withChoice

Operation.Documentation:
Adds a custom selection rule for advanced cases (conditional rules, AND/OR composition).

Use this when simple choice rules are not enough.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Adds a custom selection rule for advanced cases (conditional rules, AND/OR composition). Use this when simple choice rules are not enough.
Operation.Name: withRule

Operation.Documentation:
Returns the current ProductSet by name for use in conditional rules.

Example:

<pre>

builder.withSingleChoice("Laptops", gaming, business)

.withConditional()

.ifSelected("Laptops", gaming.id())

.thenRequired("Graphics", dedicatedGPU.id())

</pre>
Operation.Documentation_plain:
Returns the current ProductSet by name for use in conditional rules. Example: 
builder.withSingleChoice("Laptops", gaming, business)
.withConditional()
.ifSelected("Laptops", gaming.id())
.thenRequired("Graphics", dedicatedGPU.id())
Operation.Name: getProductSet

Class.Documentation:
ProductCatalog - main API for managing commercial product offering.

Accepts commands and queries with simple types, returns views.
Class.Documentation_plain: ProductCatalog - main API for managing commercial product offering. Accepts commands and queries with simple types, returns views.
Class.Name: ProductCatalog

Operation.Documentation: Adds a ProductType to the commercial offer.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Adds a ProductType to the commercial offer.
Operation.Name: handle

Operation.Documentation: Discontinues a product from the offer by setting the validity end date.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Discontinues a product from the offer by setting the validity end date.
Operation.Name: handle

Operation.Documentation: Updates catalog entry metadata.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Updates catalog entry metadata.
Operation.Name: handle

Operation.Documentation: Searches catalog entries with multiple filters.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Searches catalog entries with multiple filters.
Operation.Name: findBy

Operation.Documentation: Finds a catalog entry by its identifier.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Finds a catalog entry by its identifier.
Operation.Name: findBy

Operation.Documentation: Finds catalog entries by category.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Finds catalog entries by category.
Operation.Name: findBy

Operation.Documentation: Finds catalog entries available at specific date.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Finds catalog entries available at specific date.
Operation.Name: findBy

Operation.Documentation: Finds catalog entries by metadata key-value.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Finds catalog entries by metadata key-value.
Operation.Name: findBy

Class.Documentation:
ProductFacade - main API for managing ProductTypes.

Accepts commands and queries with simple types, returns views.
Class.Documentation_plain: ProductFacade - main API for managing ProductTypes. Accepts commands and queries with simple types, returns views.
Class.Name: ProductFacade

Operation.Documentation: Defines a new ProductType in the system.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Defines a new ProductType in the system.
Operation.Name: handle

Operation.Documentation: Finds a ProductType by its identifier.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Finds a ProductType by its identifier.
Operation.Name: findBy

Operation.Documentation: Finds ProductTypes by tracking strategy.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Finds ProductTypes by tracking strategy.
Operation.Name: findBy

Operation.Documentation:
============================================

Conversions: Simple types â†’ Domain objects

============================================
Operation.Name: parseProductIdentifier

Operation.Documentation:
============================================

Conversions: Domain objects â†’ Views

============================================
Operation.Name: toProductTypeView

Class.Documentation:
ProductFeatureInstance represents a specific feature (such as color) of a good or service

and its value (e.g., blue).

<p>

Examples:

- color: "red"

- size: "L"

- yearOfProduction: 2023

- expiryDate: 2024-12-31

<p>

Each ProductFeatureInstance:

- References its ProductFeatureType (which defines the constraint)

- Has a value that satisfies the type's constraint

- Can convert its value to/from String for persistence

<p>

The value type is validated against the feature type's constraint at construction time,

ensuring that invalid product instances cannot be created.
Class.Documentation_plain:
ProductFeatureInstance represents a specific feature (such as color) of a good or service and its value (e.g., blue).

Examples: - color: "red" - size: "L" - yearOfProduction: 2023 - expiryDate: 2024-12-31

Each ProductFeatureInstance: - References its ProductFeatureType (which defines the constraint) - Has a value that satisfies the type's constraint - Can convert its value to/from String for persistence

The value type is validated against the feature type's constraint at construction time, ensuring that invalid product instances cannot be created.
Class.Name: ProductFeatureInstance

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a ProductFeatureInstance with the given value.

The value is validated according to the feature type's constraint.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a ProductFeatureInstance with the given value. The value is validated according to the feature type's constraint.
Operation.Name: of

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a ProductFeatureInstance from a String representation.

The String is parsed and validated according to the feature type's constraint.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a ProductFeatureInstance from a String representation. The String is parsed and validated according to the feature type's constraint.
Operation.Name: fromString

Operation.Documentation:
Returns the feature value as an Object.

Use type-specific methods (asString(), asInt(), etc.) for type-safe access.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the feature value as an Object. Use type-specific methods (asString(), asInt(), etc.) for type-safe access.
Operation.Name: value

Operation.Documentation: Returns the String representation of the value (for persistence).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the String representation of the value (for persistence).
Operation.Name: valueAsString

Operation.Documentation: Returns the value as a String (if it's a text feature).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the value as a String (if it's a text feature).
Operation.Name: asString
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not a String
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not a String

Operation.Documentation: Returns the value as an Integer (if it's an integer feature).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the value as an Integer (if it's an integer feature).
Operation.Name: asInt
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not an Integer
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not an Integer

Operation.Documentation: Returns the value as a BigDecimal (if it's a decimal feature).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the value as a BigDecimal (if it's a decimal feature).
Operation.Name: asDecimal
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not a BigDecimal
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not a BigDecimal

Operation.Documentation: Returns the value as a LocalDate (if it's a date feature).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the value as a LocalDate (if it's a date feature).
Operation.Name: asDate
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not a LocalDate
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not a LocalDate

Operation.Documentation: Returns the value as a Boolean (if it's a boolean feature).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the value as a Boolean (if it's a boolean feature).
Operation.Name: asBoolean
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not a Boolean
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws IllegalStateException if the value is not a Boolean

Class.Documentation:
Container for all feature instances of a ProductInstance.

Provides convenient access to feature values by name.
Class.Documentation_plain: Container for all feature instances of a ProductInstance. Provides convenient access to feature values by name.
Class.Name: ProductFeatureInstances

Operation.Documentation: Returns the feature instance by feature name.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the feature instance by feature name.
Operation.Name: get
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: Optional containing the instance, or empty if not found
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: Optional containing the instance, or empty if not found

Operation.Documentation: Returns the feature instance by feature name.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the feature instance by feature name.
Operation.Name: get
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: Optional containing the instance, or empty if not found
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: Optional containing the instance, or empty if not found

Operation.Documentation: Checks if a feature with the given name exists.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if a feature with the given name exists.
Operation.Name: has

Operation.Documentation: Checks if a feature  exists.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if a feature exists.
Operation.Name: has

Operation.Documentation: Returns all feature instances.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns all feature instances.
Operation.Name: all

Operation.Documentation: Returns the number of feature instances.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the number of feature instances.
Operation.Name: size

Operation.Documentation: Validates that all mandatory features from ProductType are present.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Validates that all mandatory features from ProductType are present.
Operation.Name: validateAgainst
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws IllegalArgumentException if any mandatory feature is missing
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws IllegalArgumentException if any mandatory feature is missing

Class.Documentation:
ProductFeatureType represents a type of feature (such as color) of a good or service

and defines the constraint on possible values.

<p>

Examples:

- "color" with allowed values: {red, blue, black, white}

- "size" with allowed values: {S, M, L, XL}

- "yearOfProduction" with numeric range: 2020-2024

- "expiryDate" with date range: 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

<p>

Each ProductFeatureType defines:

- A unique identifier (name)

- A constraint that defines the value type and validation rules

<p>

This archetype allows for flexible specification of product features without

having to create new attributes or subclasses for each feature type.
Class.Documentation_plain:
ProductFeatureType represents a type of feature (such as color) of a good or service and defines the constraint on possible values.

Examples: - "color" with allowed values: {red, blue, black, white} - "size" with allowed values: {S, M, L, XL} - "yearOfProduction" with numeric range: 2020-2024 - "expiryDate" with date range: 2024-01-01 to 2024-12-31

Each ProductFeatureType defines: - A unique identifier (name) - A constraint that defines the value type and validation rules

This archetype allows for flexible specification of product features without having to create new attributes or subclasses for each feature type.
Class.Name: ProductFeatureType

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a ProductFeatureType with allowed text values.

Example: color with values {red, blue, green}
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a ProductFeatureType with allowed text values. Example: color with values {red, blue, green}
Operation.Name: withAllowedValues

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a ProductFeatureType with a numeric range constraint.

Example: year of production between 2020 and 2024
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a ProductFeatureType with a numeric range constraint. Example: year of production between 2020 and 2024
Operation.Name: withNumericRange

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a ProductFeatureType with a decimal range constraint.

Example: weight between 0.5 and 100.0
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a ProductFeatureType with a decimal range constraint. Example: weight between 0.5 and 100.0
Operation.Name: withDecimalRange

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a ProductFeatureType with a regex pattern constraint.

Example: product code matching "^[A-Z]{2}-\d{4}$"
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a ProductFeatureType with a regex pattern constraint. Example: product code matching "^[A-Z]{2}-\d{4}$"
Operation.Name: withRegex

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a ProductFeatureType with a date range constraint.

Example: expiry date between 2024-01-01 and 2024-12-31
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a ProductFeatureType with a date range constraint. Example: expiry date between 2024-01-01 and 2024-12-31
Operation.Name: withDateRange

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a ProductFeatureType with no constraints (any value of the specified type is valid).

Example: any text for a free-form comment
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a ProductFeatureType with no constraints (any value of the specified type is valid). Example: any text for a free-form comment
Operation.Name: unconstrained

Operation.Documentation: Creates a ProductFeatureType with a custom constraint.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a ProductFeatureType with a custom constraint.
Operation.Name: of

Operation.Documentation: Validates whether the given value is valid for this feature type.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Validates whether the given value is valid for this feature type.
Operation.Name: isValidValue
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: true if the value is valid
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: true if the value is valid

Operation.Documentation:
Validates that the given value is valid for this feature type.

Throws an exception if the value is invalid.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Validates that the given value is valid for this feature type. Throws an exception if the value is invalid.
Operation.Name: validateValue
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: @throws IllegalArgumentException if the value is not valid
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: @throws IllegalArgumentException if the value is not valid

Class.Documentation: Defines a product feature type along with whether it's mandatory or optional.
Class.Documentation_plain: Defines a product feature type along with whether it's mandatory or optional.
Class.Name: ProductFeatureTypeDefinition

Class.Documentation:
Container for all feature type definitions of a ProductType.

Provides convenient access to features by name and filtering by mandatory/optional status.
Class.Documentation_plain: Container for all feature type definitions of a ProductType. Provides convenient access to features by name and filtering by mandatory/optional status.
Class.Name: ProductFeatureTypes

Operation.Documentation: Returns the feature type definition by name.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the feature type definition by name.
Operation.Name: get
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: Optional containing the definition, or empty if not found
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: Optional containing the definition, or empty if not found

Operation.Documentation: Returns the feature type by name.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the feature type by name.
Operation.Name: getFeatureType
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: Optional containing the feature type, or empty if not found
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: Optional containing the feature type, or empty if not found

Operation.Documentation: Checks if a feature with the given name exists.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if a feature with the given name exists.
Operation.Name: has

Operation.Documentation: Checks if a feature is mandatory.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if a feature is mandatory.
Operation.Name: isMandatory
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation: true if the feature exists and is mandatory, false otherwise
Operation.ReturnTypeDocumentation_plain: true if the feature exists and is mandatory, false otherwise

Operation.Documentation: Returns all mandatory feature types.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns all mandatory feature types.
Operation.Name: mandatoryFeatures

Operation.Documentation: Returns all optional feature types.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns all optional feature types.
Operation.Name: optionalFeatures

Operation.Documentation: Returns all feature types (both mandatory and optional).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns all feature types (both mandatory and optional).
Operation.Name: allFeatures

Operation.Documentation: Returns the number of feature types.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the number of feature types.
Operation.Name: size

Class.Documentation:
ProductInstance represents a specific instance/exemplar of a ProductType.

Examples:

- ProductType: "iPhone 15 Pro 256GB" -> ProductInstance: specific phone with serial ABC123

- ProductType: "Clean Code book" -> ProductInstance: specific book copy

- ProductType: "Organic Milk 1L" -> ProductInstance: specific bottle from batch LOT-2024-001

- ProductType: "Consulting" -> ProductInstance: 8.5 hours of consulting delivered

Each instance must be tracked by at least one of:

- SerialNumber (individual tracking)

- Batch (group tracking for quality control)

- Or both (e.g., high-value items in batches like TVs)

Optional quantity tracks the amount for this specific instance (e.g., 8.5 hours, 3.2 kg).

If not specified, quantity is implicitly 1 unit of the ProductType's preferred unit.

ProductInstance can have features that specify the actual values for features

defined in the ProductType (e.g., color=red, size=L, yearOfProduction=2023).
Class.Documentation_plain:
ProductInstance represents a specific instance/exemplar of a ProductType.
Examples: - ProductType: "iPhone 15 Pro 256GB" -> ProductInstance: specific phone with serial ABC123 - ProductType: "Clean Code book" -> ProductInstance: specific book copy - ProductType: "Organic Milk 1L" -> ProductInstance: specific bottle from batch LOT-2024-001 - ProductType: "Consulting" -> ProductInstance: 8.5 hours of consulting delivered
Each instance must be tracked by at least one of: - SerialNumber (individual tracking) - Batch (group tracking for quality control) - Or both (e.g., high-value items in batches like TVs)
Optional quantity tracks the amount for this specific instance (e.g., 8.5 hours, 3.2 kg). If not specified, quantity is implicitly 1 unit of the ProductType's preferred unit.
ProductInstance can have features that specify the actual values for features defined in the ProductType (e.g., color=red, size=L, yearOfProduction=2023).
Class.Name: ProductInstance

Operation.Documentation:
Returns the effective quantity of this instance.

If explicit quantity is set, returns it.

Otherwise returns implicit "1 unit" of the product's preferred unit.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns the effective quantity of this instance. If explicit quantity is set, returns it. Otherwise returns implicit "1 unit" of the product's preferred unit.
Operation.Name: effectiveQuantity

Class.Documentation:
ProductMetadata represents static, unchanging properties of a ProductType.

These are simple key-value pairs that describe fixed characteristics of the product type.

Examples:

- category: "coffee", "electronics", "clothing"

- seasonal: "true", "false"

- season: "winter", "summer"

- brand: "Apple", "Samsung"

- material: "cotton", "polyester"
Class.Documentation_plain:
ProductMetadata represents static, unchanging properties of a ProductType. These are simple key-value pairs that describe fixed characteristics of the product type.
Examples: - category: "coffee", "electronics", "clothing" - seasonal: "true", "false" - season: "winter", "summer" - brand: "Apple", "Samsung" - material: "cotton", "polyester"
Class.Name: ProductMetadata

Operation.Documentation: Defines a new relationship between two products.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Defines a new relationship between two products.
Operation.Name: handle

Operation.Documentation: Removes an existing relationship.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Removes an existing relationship.
Operation.Name: handle

Operation.Documentation:
============================================

Conversions: Simple types â†’ Domain objects

============================================
Operation.Name: parseProductIdentifier

Class.Documentation: Wszystkie relacje sÄ… asymetryczne (skierowane): from â†’ to
Class.Documentation_plain: Wszystkie relacje sÄ… asymetryczne (skierowane): from â†’ to
Class.Name: ProductRelationshipType

Class.Documentation:
ProductSet represents a named collection of products available for selection in a package.

This is the "raw material" - a pool of product options without any selection constraints.

<p>

Example: A laptop package might have ProductSets like "Memory Options" (4GB, 8GB, 16GB),

"Storage Options" (256GB SSD, 512GB SSD, 1TB SSD), "Accessories" (mouse, keyboard, bag).
Class.Documentation_plain:
ProductSet represents a named collection of products available for selection in a package. This is the "raw material" - a pool of product options without any selection constraints.

Example: A laptop package might have ProductSets like "Memory Options" (4GB, 8GB, 16GB), "Storage Options" (256GB SSD, 512GB SSD, 1TB SSD), "Accessories" (mouse, keyboard, bag).
Class.Name: ProductSet

Class.Documentation:
Defines how product instances are tracked and identified in the system.

This directly addresses the fundamental question: "How do we distinguish

between individual items of this product type?"
Class.Documentation_plain:
Defines how product instances are tracked and identified in the system.
This directly addresses the fundamental question: "How do we distinguish between individual items of this product type?"
Class.Name: ProductTrackingStrategy

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
UNIQUE - One-of-a-kind product.

Example: Hetfield's guitar, da Vinci's painting
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: UNIQUE - One-of-a-kind product. Example: Hetfield's guitar, da Vinci's painting
EnumerationLiteral.Name: UNIQUE

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
INDIVIDUALLY_TRACKED - Each instance uniquely identified.

Examples: iPhone, mortgage contract, parcel tracking
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: INDIVIDUALLY_TRACKED - Each instance uniquely identified. Examples: iPhone, mortgage contract, parcel tracking
EnumerationLiteral.Name: INDIVIDUALLY_TRACKED

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
BATCH_TRACKED - Tracked by production batch for quality control.

Examples: milk bottles, pharmaceuticals, food products
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: BATCH_TRACKED - Tracked by production batch for quality control. Examples: milk bottles, pharmaceuticals, food products
EnumerationLiteral.Name: BATCH_TRACKED

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
INDIVIDUALLY_AND_BATCH_TRACKED - Both individual and batch tracking.

Examples: TVs, smartphones (serial + batch for recalls)
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: INDIVIDUALLY_AND_BATCH_TRACKED - Both individual and batch tracking. Examples: TVs, smartphones (serial + batch for recalls)
EnumerationLiteral.Name: INDIVIDUALLY_AND_BATCH_TRACKED

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation:
IDENTICAL - Interchangeable items, may or may not create instances.

Examples: screws (bulk), rice bags (instance per bag), flour (bulk)
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: IDENTICAL - Interchangeable items, may or may not create instances. Examples: screws (bulk), rice bags (instance per bag), flour (bulk)
EnumerationLiteral.Name: IDENTICAL

Operation.Documentation: Returns true if each instance must have unique individual identity.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns true if each instance must have unique individual identity.
Operation.Name: isTrackedIndividually

Operation.Documentation: Returns true if instances are tracked by production batch.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns true if instances are tracked by production batch.
Operation.Name: isTrackedByBatch

Operation.Documentation: Returns true if both individual and batch tracking is required.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns true if both individual and batch tracking is required.
Operation.Name: requiresBothTrackingMethods

Operation.Documentation: Returns true if instances are interchangeable (no unique identity needed).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns true if instances are interchangeable (no unique identity needed).
Operation.Name: isInterchangeable

Class.Documentation:
ProductType represents a type/definition of a product (not a specific instance).

Examples: "iPhone 15 Pro 256GB", "Clean Code book", "Organic Milk 1L"

Each ProductType is uniquely identified by a ProductIdentifier (ISBN, GTIN, UUID, etc.)

and defines how instances should be tracked and measured.

ProductType can define mandatory and optional feature types that instances must or may have.

This is a leaf in the composite pattern - a regular product (not a package).
Class.Documentation_plain:
ProductType represents a type/definition of a product (not a specific instance). Examples: "iPhone 15 Pro 256GB", "Clean Code book", "Organic Milk 1L"
Each ProductType is uniquely identified by a ProductIdentifier (ISBN, GTIN, UUID, etc.) and defines how instances should be tracked and measured.
ProductType can define mandatory and optional feature types that instances must or may have.
This is a leaf in the composite pattern - a regular product (not a package).
Class.Name: ProductType

Operation.Documentation: Creates a simple product type for testing purposes.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a simple product type for testing purposes.
Operation.Name: define

Operation.Documentation:
Creates a unique product type (one-of-a-kind).

Unit is implicitly pieces, as there's always exactly 1 exemplar.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a unique product type (one-of-a-kind). Unit is implicitly pieces, as there's always exactly 1 exemplar.
Operation.Name: unique

Operation.Documentation: Creates a product type where each instance is individually tracked (e.g., by serial number).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a product type where each instance is individually tracked (e.g., by serial number).
Operation.Name: individuallyTracked

Operation.Documentation: Creates a product type where instances are tracked by production batch.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a product type where instances are tracked by production batch.
Operation.Name: batchTracked

Operation.Documentation: Creates a product type where instances are tracked both individually and by batch.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a product type where instances are tracked both individually and by batch.
Operation.Name: individuallyAndBatchTracked

Operation.Documentation: Creates a product type where instances are interchangeable (identical).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates a product type where instances are interchangeable (identical).
Operation.Name: identical

Operation.Documentation:
Returns a builder for ProductType with specified tracking and measurement parameters.

This is a convenience method that delegates to Product.builder().asProductType().
Operation.Documentation_plain: Returns a builder for ProductType with specified tracking and measurement parameters. This is a convenience method that delegates to Product.builder().asProductType().
Operation.Name: builder

Class.Documentation: Repository for ProductType persistence.
Class.Documentation_plain: Repository for ProductType persistence.
Class.Name: ProductTypeRepository

Class.Documentation: In-memory implementation of ProductTypeRepository.
Class.Documentation_plain: In-memory implementation of ProductTypeRepository.
Class.Name: InMemoryProductTypeRepository

Class.Documentation:
Validates text values against a regular expression pattern.

Example: product code must match pattern "^[A-Z]{2}-\d{4}$"

Persistence config example: {"pattern": "^[A-Z]{2}-\\d{4}$"}
Class.Name: RegexConstraint

Class.Documentation:
Records the status of the delivery of a particular ServiceInstance.

ServiceInstances are executions of a process, with a lifecycle:

SCHEDULED â†’ EXECUTING â†’ COMPLETED/CANCELLED
Class.Documentation_plain:
Records the status of the delivery of a particular ServiceInstance.
ServiceInstances are executions of a process, with a lifecycle: SCHEDULED â†’ EXECUTING â†’ COMPLETED/CANCELLED
Class.Name: ServiceDeliveryStatus

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation: The ServiceInstance has been scheduled for delivery.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: The ServiceInstance has been scheduled for delivery.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: SCHEDULED

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation: The ServiceInstance is currently in the process of delivery.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: The ServiceInstance is currently in the process of delivery.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: EXECUTING

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation: The delivery of the ServiceInstance has been completed successfully.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: The delivery of the ServiceInstance has been completed successfully.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: COMPLETED

EnumerationLiteral.Documentation: The ServiceInstance has been cancelled before or during execution.
EnumerationLiteral.Documentation_plain: The ServiceInstance has been cancelled before or during execution.
EnumerationLiteral.Name: CANCELLED

Class.Documentation:
No constraints - any value of the specified type is valid.

Example: any text for a free-form comment field

Persistence config example: {} (empty)
Class.Documentation_plain:
No constraints - any value of the specified type is valid. Example: any text for a free-form comment field
Persistence config example: {} (empty)
Class.Name: Unconstrained

Class.Documentation:
Represents a time period during which something is valid (available, active, etc.).

Both boundaries are optional:

- No from date = valid since the beginning of time

- No to date = valid indefinitely
Class.Documentation_plain: Represents a time period during which something is valid (available, active, etc.). Both boundaries are optional: - No from date = valid since the beginning of time - No to date = valid indefinitely
Class.Name: Validity

Operation.Documentation: Creates validity period from given date (inclusive) with no end date.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates validity period from given date (inclusive) with no end date.
Operation.Name: from

Operation.Documentation: Creates validity period until given date (inclusive) with no start date.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates validity period until given date (inclusive) with no start date.
Operation.Name: until

Operation.Documentation: Creates validity period between two dates (both inclusive).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates validity period between two dates (both inclusive).
Operation.Name: between

Operation.Documentation: Creates validity period with no boundaries (always valid).
Operation.Documentation_plain: Creates validity period with no boundaries (always valid).
Operation.Name: always

Operation.Documentation: Checks if the given date falls within this validity period.
Operation.Documentation_plain: Checks if the given date falls within this validity period.
Operation.Name: isValidAt

Class.Documentation:
Scenario from lesson: ApplicabilityConstraint examples

Demonstrates products with business rules that restrict their availability

based on context (channel, country, age, etc.)

Examples:

- Mobile-only products (only available on mobile channel)

- Pediatric products (only for children under 16 in PL/UK)

- Age-restricted products (teenagers only)

- Multi-dimensional constraints (country AND channel AND age)
Class.Documentation_plain:
Scenario from lesson: ApplicabilityConstraint examples
Demonstrates products with business rules that restrict their availability based on context (channel, country, age, etc.)
Examples: - Mobile-only products (only available on mobile channel) - Pediatric products (only for children under 16 in PL/UK) - Age-restricted products (teenagers only) - Multi-dimensional constraints (country AND channel AND age)
Class.Name: ApplicabilityConstraintScenario

Class.Documentation: Tests for CatalogEntry - commercial offering position.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for CatalogEntry - commercial offering position.
Class.Name: CatalogEntryTest

Class.Documentation: Tests for FeatureValueConstraint implementations.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for FeatureValueConstraint implementations.
Class.Name: FeatureValueConstraintTest

Class.Documentation: Tests for InstanceBuilder and the unified building approach for ProductInstance and PackageInstance.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for InstanceBuilder and the unified building approach for ProductInstance and PackageInstance.
Class.Name: InstanceBuilderTest

Class.Documentation:
Scenario from lesson: Logistics Transport Premium Package

<p>

A logistics company offers Transport Premium - a comprehensive service including:

- Transport (domestic or international)

- Insurance (standard, extended, or partner)

- Add-ons (cold chain, fragile handling, pickup service)

- Tracking Package (system tracking or active monitoring)

- Notification Package (SMS, email, webhook notifications)

<p>

Business rule: Standard insurance cannot be selected for international transport
Class.Documentation_plain:
Scenario from lesson: Logistics Transport Premium Package

A logistics company offers Transport Premium - a comprehensive service including: - Transport (domestic or international) - Insurance (standard, extended, or partner) - Add-ons (cold chain, fragile handling, pickup service) - Tracking Package (system tracking or active monitoring) - Notification Package (SMS, email, webhook notifications)

Business rule: Standard insurance cannot be selected for international transport
Class.Name: LogisticsTransportScenario

Class.Documentation: Tests for PackageInstance creation and validation.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for PackageInstance creation and validation.
Class.Name: PackageInstanceTest

Class.Documentation: Tests for PackageType creation and selection validation.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for PackageType creation and selection validation.
Class.Name: PackageTypeTest

Class.Documentation: Tests for ProductCatalog - main API for managing commercial product offering.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for ProductCatalog - main API for managing commercial product offering.
Class.Name: ProductCatalogTest

Class.Documentation: Tests for ProductFacade - main API for managing ProductTypes.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for ProductFacade - main API for managing ProductTypes.
Class.Name: ProductFacadeTest

Operation.Documentation:
===========================================

Finding by tracking strategy

===========================================
Operation.Name: shouldFindProductTypesByTrackingStrategy

Operation.Documentation:
===========================================

Different identifier types

===========================================
Operation.Name: shouldSupportIsbnIdentifier

Class.Documentation:
Scenario: Product Relationships in real business contexts

Demonstrates various relationship types between products:

- UPGRADABLE_TO: Premium versions, higher tiers

- SUBSTITUTED_BY: Alternative products, replacements

- REPLACED_BY: Discontinued products replaced by new versions

- COMPLEMENTED_BY: Products that work together

- COMPATIBLE_WITH: Technical compatibility

- INCOMPATIBLE_WITH: Products that cannot be used together
Class.Documentation_plain:
Scenario: Product Relationships in real business contexts
Demonstrates various relationship types between products: - UPGRADABLE_TO: Premium versions, higher tiers - SUBSTITUTED_BY: Alternative products, replacements - REPLACED_BY: Discontinued products replaced by new versions - COMPLEMENTED_BY: Products that work together - COMPATIBLE_WITH: Technical compatibility - INCOMPATIBLE_WITH: Products that cannot be used together
Class.Name: ProductRelationshipsScenario

Class.Documentation: Tests for SelectedInstance record.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for SelectedInstance record.
Class.Name: SelectedInstanceTest

Class.Documentation: Tests for SelectionRule implementations.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for SelectionRule implementations.
Class.Name: SelectionRuleTest

Operation.Documentation: IsSubsetOf tests
Operation.Documentation_plain: IsSubsetOf tests
Operation.Name: isSubsetOf_shouldAcceptSelectionWithinRange

Operation.Documentation: Required tests
Operation.Documentation_plain: Required tests
Operation.Name: required_shouldAcceptSelectionWithProduct

Operation.Documentation: Optional tests
Operation.Documentation_plain: Optional tests
Operation.Name: optional_shouldAcceptBothWithAndWithoutProduct

Operation.Documentation: Single tests
Operation.Documentation_plain: Single tests
Operation.Name: single_shouldAcceptExactlyOne

Operation.Documentation: AndRule tests
Operation.Documentation_plain: AndRule tests
Operation.Name: and_shouldRequireAllRulesToBeSatisfied

Operation.Documentation: OrRule tests
Operation.Documentation_plain: OrRule tests
Operation.Name: or_shouldRequireAtLeastOneRuleToBeSatisfied

Operation.Documentation: NotRule tests
Operation.Documentation_plain: NotRule tests
Operation.Name: not_shouldInvertRuleResult

Operation.Documentation: ConditionalRule tests
Operation.Documentation_plain: ConditionalRule tests
Operation.Name: ifThen_shouldEnforceThenRulesWhenConditionIsMet

Operation.Documentation: Complex combinations
Operation.Documentation_plain: Complex combinations
Operation.Name: shouldCombineAndOrNotInComplexScenario

Class.Documentation:
Scenario: Telecom packages with phones and plans

<p>

Real-world telecom offering:

- Mobile plans (Basic, Standard, Premium)

- Phones (budget, mid-range, flagship)

- SIM cards and accessories

- Business rules: Premium phones require Premium plans

- Nested packages: Starter packs with SIM + accessories
Class.Documentation_plain:
Scenario: Telecom packages with phones and plans

Real-world telecom offering: - Mobile plans (Basic, Standard, Premium) - Phones (budget, mid-range, flagship) - SIM cards and accessories - Business rules: Premium phones require Premium plans - Nested packages: Starter packs with SIM + accessories
Class.Name: TelecomPackageScenario

Class.Documentation:
Scenario from lesson: ProductTrackingStrategy examples

Demonstrates how different tracking strategies affect product instances:

- UNIQUE: One-of-a-kind products (Hetfield's guitar, da Vinci's painting)

- INDIVIDUALLY_TRACKED: Each instance uniquely identified (iPhone, parcel)

- BATCH_TRACKED: Tracked by production batch (milk, pharmaceuticals)

- INDIVIDUALLY_AND_BATCH_TRACKED: Both tracking methods (TV, smartphone)

- IDENTICAL: Interchangeable items (screws, rice)
Class.Documentation_plain:
Scenario from lesson: ProductTrackingStrategy examples
Demonstrates how different tracking strategies affect product instances:
- UNIQUE: One-of-a-kind products (Hetfield's guitar, da Vinci's painting) - INDIVIDUALLY_TRACKED: Each instance uniquely identified (iPhone, parcel) - BATCH_TRACKED: Tracked by production batch (milk, pharmaceuticals) - INDIVIDUALLY_AND_BATCH_TRACKED: Both tracking methods (TV, smartphone) - IDENTICAL: Interchangeable items (screws, rice)
Class.Name: TrackingStrategyScenario

Class.Documentation: Tests for Validity - time period during which something is valid.
Class.Documentation_plain: Tests for Validity - time period during which something is valid.
Class.Name: ValidityTest
